summary: Prime Minister Netanyahu presented the Israel-Lebanon agreement as a historic achievement and a major blow to Iran and Hezbollah, stating it could end the conflict. An intruder alert in Beit Arieh settlement in the Benjamin division was declared over after extensive IDF searches found no suspects. Iranian state media reported 63 members of the Assembly of Experts signed a statement calling the Strait of Hormuz reopening a violation of commitments, while Bahrain accused Iran of drone attacks on its territory.
key_points: Netanyahu presented the Israel-Lebanon agreement as a historic achievement and a strategic blow to the Iranian axis, stating it could end the conflict. (Source: @amitsegal/62561, @amitsegal/62562, @yediotnews25/131980)

Defense Minister Katz said the IDF will maintain its security zone in Lebanon including the Beaufort Castle ridge area, and that no withdrawal will occur as long as Hezbollah remains armed. (Source: @manniefabian/58687)

An intruder alert in Beit Arieh settlement (Benjamin division) was declared over after extensive IDF and Shin Bet searches found no suspects. (Source: @idf_telegram/24451, @idf_telegram/24452, @almogboker78/17603)

IDF and Shin Bet announced the elimination of Walid al-Nahia, deputy commander of a Nukhba company who commanded the October 7 infiltration into Israel. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93274, @yediotnews25/131979)
Iran's Assembly of Experts members signed a statement calling the Strait of Hormuz reopening a violation of the Iran-US agreement and a strategic error. (Source: @VahidOnline/76713)

Bahrain's Foreign Ministry stated Iran attacked its territory with drones early Saturday, calling it a violation of sovereignty and the Islamabad agreement with the US. (Source: @VahidOnline/76707, @shin_persian/7233)
IRGC naval forces fired warning shots at violating vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373205)
Netanyahu stated he intends to form a broad national government and that there is no place for two states between the sea and the Jordan River. (Source: @pkpoi/142999, @presstv/195896)

sections.executive_summary: Prime Minister Netanyahu presented the Israel-Lebanon agreement as a historic achievement and a strategic blow to Iran and Hezbollah, stating it could end the conflict. Defense Minister Katz confirmed the IDF will maintain a security zone in southern Lebanon until Hezbollah is disarmed. Meanwhile, Iran faced internal criticism from Assembly of Experts members over the Strait of Hormuz reopening, and Bahrain accused Iran of drone attacks on its territory.

points: Iran's state media reported that 63 members of the Assembly of Experts signed a collective statement calling the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz a violation of officials' commitments and a strategic error. The statement, published by Tasnim and Fars news agencies, said continued Israeli attacks on Lebanon and the IDF's non-withdrawal from southern Lebanese areas constituted a clear violation of the Iran-US agreement. (Source: @VahidOnline/76713)

The Assembly of Experts members listed demands including stabilization of Strait of Hormuz management, receipt of compensation for damages, return of blocked assets, lifting of sanctions, and US withdrawal from the region. They warned that any negligence in these matters would be met with a response. (Source: @VahidOnline/76713)
IRGC naval forces fired warning shots at violating vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373205)
Iranian air defense missiles were launched in the sky over Qeshm Island. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373206)

An Iranian official said the agreement with the US was approved by over 90% of the Supreme National Security Council members, including senior IRGC commanders, and negotiations for its implementation have begun. (Source: @VahidOnline/76713)
The representative of the Supreme Leader in the IRGC warned critics of the agreement not to create divisions that could be exploited by the enemy. (Source: @VahidOnline/76713)

Prime Minister Netanyahu held a press conference at 20:45 after the framework agreement with Lebanon, calling it a historic achievement that strengthens Israel and Lebanon and constitutes a major blow to Iran and Hezbollah. (Source: @almogboker78/17607, @amitsegal/62561, @pkpoi/142998)

Defense Minister Katz said the IDF will not fully withdraw from Lebanon and will maintain its security zone including the Beaufort Castle ridge area. He stated that no withdrawal will occur as long as Hezbollah remains armed throughout Lebanon, and instructed the military to prepare for a prolonged stay. (Source: @manniefabian/58687)

An intruder alert was activated overnight in Beit Arieh settlement in the Benjamin division following the identification of two suspects. Extensive IDF searches were conducted but no suspects were found, and the event was declared over. (Source: @idf_telegram/24451, @idf_telegram/24452, @almogboker78/17603)

IDF and Shin Bet announced the elimination of Walid al-Nahia, nephew of Ismail al-Nahia, who was deputy commander of a Nukhba company and commanded the October 7 infiltration into Israeli territory. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93274, @yediotnews25/131979)
Netanyahu stated he intends to form a broad national government and that there is no place for two states between the sea and the Jordan River. (Source: @pkpoi/142999, @presstv/195896)

Beit Arieh council head Sharon Alfasi said the settlement was running continuous situation assessments with security forces for over seven hours and called for increased security budgets for settlements in Judea and Samaria. (Source: @almogboker78/17605)

An intruder alert was activated in Beit Arieh settlement in the Benjamin division following identification of two masked suspects outside a resident's home around 4:00 AM. The suspects fled when called to, prompting a military curfew and extensive searches by forces from the brigade and Border Police. (Source: @inon_yttach/9178, @idf_telegram/24451)

Beit Arieh head council member Sharon Alfasi coordinated with IDF, Israel Police, and all relevant security forces throughout the event, maintaining continuous contact with settlement residents. (Source: @almogboker78/17605)
Netanyahu stated there is no place for two states between the sea and the Jordan River. (Source: @presstv/195896)

Prime Minister Netanyahu presented the Israel-Lebanon agreement as a historic achievement that could end the conflict, calling it a major blow to Iran and Hezbollah. (Source: @amitsegal/62561, @yediotnews25/131980)

Defense Minister Katz said the agreement may for the first time in decades shape a new and safer reality along the northern border and in Lebanon, and that the IDF will maintain its security zone. (Source: @manniefabian/58687)

Lebanon's judicial system called for security forces to deal firmly with Hezbollah-affiliated rioters. The Prosecutor General of Lebanon asked security and military agency heads to take necessary measures to prevent disturbances and roadblocks by protesters deceived by the agreement with Israel. (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74430)
Hezbollah supporters protested in Beirut following the agreement. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93259)
Pro-Hezbollah billboards on the road to Beirut airport were replaced with 'Lebanon First' billboards. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93272)
No specific operational developments regarding Syria or Iraq were reported in the provided messages.

Bahrain's Foreign Ministry stated in a communique that the Islamic Republic attacked its territory with several drones early Saturday morning, calling it a violation of Bahrain's sovereignty and Iran's commitments under the Islamabad agreement with the US. (Source: @VahidOnline/76707, @shin_persian/7233)

An unnamed US official told the Wall Street Journal that Iran's attack on Bahrain early Saturday involved two suicide drones. One was shot down by a ground-based air defense system, and the other landed without causing damage or casualties at a remote airport. A suicide drone also struck a tanker carrying two million barrels of crude oil, and US forces shot down two additional drones targeting commercial vessels. (Source: @VahidOnline/76707)
The UAE strongly condemned Iran's hostile drone attacks on Bahrain. (Source: @shin_persian/7233)
The UAE Foreign Ministry described the attacks as a flagrant violation of Bahrain's sovereignty and a threat to regional security. (Source: @VahidOnline/76707)

Qatar's Foreign Ministry condemned the attacks and emphasized the need to avoid consequences of these unwarranted attacks and continue the path of diplomacy. Kuwait called the attacks dangerously undermining peace efforts and affirmed full solidarity with Bahrain. (Source: @VahidOnline/76707)
GCC Secretary-General Jassim Mohammed Al-Budawi condemned the attacks as treacherous, stating they targeted civilian infrastructure. (Source: @VahidOnline/76707)
The IRGC targeted a Singaporean cargo ship two nights prior, contributing to a severely critical security environment in the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @VahidOnline/76707)

key_developments: Prime Minister Netanyahu presented the Israel-Lebanon framework agreement as a historic achievement and a strategic blow to Iran and Hezbollah, stating it could end the conflict. (Source: @amitsegal/62561, @yediotnews25/131980)

Defense Minister Katz confirmed the IDF will maintain a security zone in Lebanon including the Beaufort Castle ridge area, with no withdrawal as long as Hezbollah remains armed. (Source: @manniefabian/58687)
IDF and Shin Bet eliminated Walid al-Nahia, deputy commander of a Nukhba company who commanded the October 7 infiltration into Israeli territory. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93274, @yediotnews25/131979)
An intruder alert in Beit Arieh settlement (Benjamin division) was declared over after extensive searches by IDF forces found no suspects. (Source: @idf_telegram/24452, @almogboker78/17603)

Bahrain accused Iran of drone attacks on its territory early Saturday, with one drone shot down and another landing at a remote airport; a tanker was also struck. (Source: @VahidOnline/76707, @shin_persian/7233)
63 members of Iran's Assembly of Experts signed a statement calling the Strait of Hormuz reopening a violation of commitments and a strategic error. (Source: @VahidOnline/76713)

IRGC naval forces fired warning shots at violating vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, and Iranian air defense missiles were launched over Qeshm Island. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373205, @rasedal3ado138e/373206)
sections.threat_assessment: The Israel-Lebanon agreement represents a significant strategic shift, with Israel securing a continued security zone presence in southern Lebanon. Iran faces internal political pressure from hardliners opposing the agreement and Strait of Hormuz reopening. The Iranian drone attacks on Bahrain, occurring alongside IRGC operations in the Strait of Hormuz, signal an escalation pattern that threatens Gulf state security and could draw in US military responses. The elimination of a senior Nukhba commander indicates continued Israeli counterterrorism operations in the West Bank.
sections.regional_response: Gulf states led by Bahrain, the UAE, Kuwait, and Qatar strongly condemned Iran's drone attacks, with the GCC Secretary-General calling them treacherous. Lebanon's judicial system moved to suppress Hezbollah-affiliated protests against the agreement. Within Iran, 63 Assembly of Experts members issued a coordinated statement opposing the agreement's terms, while the IRGC representative warned internal critics against creating divisions. The US provided intelligence to media outlets about the specifics of the Bahrain drone attack.
sections.intelligence_notes: The Bahrain drone attack involved at least two suicide drones and potentially up to four drones total according to US sources, with one striking a crude oil tanker carrying two million barrels. The attack occurred hours after the IRGC announced targeting of US military positions in response to the Sirri Island strikes. Iran's Supreme National Security Council approved the US agreement by over 90% according to President Pezeshkian, but internal opposition from the Assembly of Experts suggests implementation friction. The IDF maintained operational tempo in the West Bank with the targeted killing of a Nukhba company commander linked to the October 7 attack.
